generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

target_selection.target: a registered reference-library entry to audit

evals.prompt: Audit the reference library and tell me if anything in there is unsafe to pull patterns from.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script over the registry

judgment: Loops the audit script over every entry in registry.json for a full sweep
Presents exit-3 SECURITY findings separately from quality warnings (stale last_reviewed, thin prohibited_uses)
Reads its own report from .project-brain/reports/security/ rather than fabricating results

evals.prompt: Is the gstack source safe to use? I want to pull some patterns from it but want to make sure it's clean first.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script for the single entry

judgment: Runs the audit for just the gstack entry rather than a full sweep, matching the scoped question
Produces the same safety-gate outcome as the direct-trigger scenario despite reworded phrasing

evals.prompt: Check the reference repos — I think one of them might have an executable checked in under sources/ by mistake.

workflow_steps.step: Surface the exit-3 finding as blocking

judgment: Surfaces an executable-under-sources finding as an exit-3 SECURITY finding, not lumped in with warnings
States clearly that this exit-3 finding BLOCKS pattern-extraction work on that source until resolved
Does not offer to delete the guard or the report to make the finding go away — points at redacting the source and re-auditing

evals.prompt: Why do we even audit reference repos in the first place? What's the actual risk we're guarding against?

judgment: Correctly reads this as a conceptual/rationale question, not a request to run an audit
Does not invoke the reference-repo-audit workflow or script — answers conversationally instead

evals.prompt: Before we mine that new source for ideas, can you make sure it doesn't have any red flags — installable scripts, secrets, wrong policy settings, anything like that?

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script for the named source

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('red flags', 'installable scripts', 'secrets', 'policy settings') activates the skill without the literal word 'audit'
Correctly frames this as the pre-extraction gate check rather than the extraction itself

evals.prompt: Register github.com/foo/bar as a new reference source — it looks like a solid pattern library we should start tracking.

judgment: Adversarial probe using registry vocabulary ('reference source', 'tracking') that names reference-repo-add's job, not reference-repo-audit's
Must NOT invoke reference-repo-audit's workflow — a conversational redirect toward reference-repo-add is correct

evals.prompt: Run a periodic review of the reference library for this Node.js toolkit — I want to know before skill-scout pulls anything else in whether our existing sources are still clean.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script over the registry

judgment: Recognizes 'periodic library review' as the full-sweep audit mode described in the skill's workflow
Integrates with the project's Node.js stack (script invocation, no build step)
Frames the audit as a precondition for future skill-scout / extraction work, not a one-off

evals.prompt: Before running reference-repo-pattern-extract on the gstack source, first audit it and confirm there's no open exit-3 finding blocking us.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script for the gstack entry

judgment: Activates correctly within the reference-repo-audit -> reference-repo-pattern-extract handoff in the project's pipeline
Reports exit code / clean status explicitly so the extraction gate decision is unambiguous
Does not itself perform the pattern extraction — that is reference-repo-pattern-extract's job

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We discussed claude_code and agreed I'd be auditing the 'zustand' reference-library entry we just registered. We're using SKILL (our hooks include log-invocation.js, log-outcome.js). Let's continue — go ahead and run that audit now.

workflow_steps.step: Run the audit script for the named entry

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing
Does not re-ask which entry to audit — uses the already-established name='zustand' directly
Correctly treats this as the natural follow-on to a prior registration, not a duplicate registration itself
